title: Buildah looks for USER in /etc/subuid but not UID
body:
According to the manpage, using UID instead of USER is valid for `/etc/sub{u,g}id`. More than just valid - it's recommended:
```console
$ man subuid
...
       ·   login name or UID

       When large number of entries (10000-100000 or more) are defined in /etc/subuid, parsing performance penalty will become noticeable. In this case it is recommended to use UIDs instead of login names. Benchmarks have shown speed-ups up to 20x.
```

But buildah (which relies on `github.com/containers/storage/pkg/idtools` to search through the files) only looks for USER, not UID:
```console
$ echo "$(id -u):100000:65536" | sudo tee /etc/subuid
$ echo "$(id -u):100000:65536" | sudo tee /etc/subgid
$ buildah --debug=true pull docker://docker.io/library/ubuntu
WARN[0000] error reading allowed ID mappings: error reading subuid mappings for user "lyeager" and subgid mappings for group "lyeager": No subuid ranges found for user "lyeager" in /etc/subuid                                                                                 
WARN[0000] Found no UID ranges set aside for user "lyeager" in /etc/subuid.                                                                                                                                                                                                      
WARN[0000] Found no GID ranges set aside for user "lyeager" in /etc/subgid.                                            
```
When I use `$(id -un)` instead, the warnings go away.

comments.body: With the above patch applied to buildah, it works with UID specified in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid.
Nice, thanks for the quick response!
